steps.title: Map the passage structure
steps.content: The passage first explains a trait shared by all baleen whales: they filter krill through baleen plates and can consume large amounts. It then compares humpback and fin whales in size, a difference related to how much krill they can eat.

steps.title: Keep the order accurate
steps.content: Choice A reverses the order by putting the size distinction first. Choice B invents population changes. Choice C treats baleen feeding as unique to humpbacks, even though the passage says all baleen whales share it. The remaining choice matches both the sequence and the comparison. The answer is D.
